"'''Luke Skywalker's Walkabout'''" is a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written and drawn by [[Phillip Norwood|Phill Norwood]]. It was first published in the [[1999]] anthology ''[[Dark Horse Presents Annual 1999: DHP Jr.]]''. It was later reprinted in color in the [[2008]] collection ''[[Star Wars: Luke Skywalker, Last Hope for the Galaxy]]''.

==Publisher's summary==
''Though Luke Skywalker feels that his life on a moisture farm on Tatooine is boring, the events seen in ''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'' are neither young Luke's first adventure, nor his first meeting with the mysterious Ben Kenobi. In this story, based on events in the ''Star Wars Radio Drama'', Luke and his friend Windy learn for the first time that the Jundland Wastes are not to be traveled lightly...''

==Plot summary==
Around [[6 BBY/Legends|6 BBY]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Windy Starkiller]] attempt a journey to [[Ja-Mero Ridge/Legends|Ja-Mero Ridge]], but they get held up by a [[Sandstorm/Legends|sandswirl]]. Then [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Ben Kenobi]] rescues them and guides them back to the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars homestead]].

==Continuity==
This story, originally black and white, was printed in color in ''[[Star Wars: Luke Skywalker, Last Hope for the Galaxy]]''. In the original, [[Windy Starkiller|Windy]] is referred to as both Windom Starkiller and Windy Marstrap (one can assume that Windy is short for Windom). In the reprint, his last name has been corrected; it is mentioned as ''Starkiller'' both times.
